item.title: CoPiglet Update: Hardware
item.description: Progress has slowed, and likely won’t be getting faster any time soon, and that’s OK. My last blog post on what I’m now calling the CoPiglet (like copilot, but a pig, get it?) focused on the Android application. I’ve held off on posting a follow-up post focusing on the hardware present in the backpack, because it’s been slowly starting to undergo some major changes. The Present The current hardware in the backpack is:

item.title: Backpack Directions - The Brain
item.description: I haven’t tested this very thoroughly, so this post is going to mostly stick to generalities. My goal for the “direction” part of the backpack direction system was to do as little work as possible. The interesting parts of this project, for me, were in conveying those directions to a user. The actual challenge of getting directions from point A to point B isn’t one that I have much interest in solving.

item.title: Arriving without dying.
item.description: How can I get somewhere I’ve never been, while not negatively impacting my safety as a cyclist? I try to be a safe biker. I wear my helmet. I use lights. I don’t look at my phone. I never, ever wear headphones. I realize that biking, even in a city with as much bike infrastructure as Montreal, has some inherent dangers, so I do my best to make sure that I’m not limiting myself by blocking off what I’m hearing.
